justification: Meets all pass criteria on substance — PM/Legal contradiction explicitly identified and escalated (Contradiction 1, Rejection R-1), clock-start ambiguity identified (Gap G-1, Rejection R-3), wrong-address exception from Finance noted (BR-7, Contradiction 3), auto-refund vs 3-day processing conflict noted (Contradiction 2), 11 rules extracted with correct invariant classifications, 3 formal escalations via Rejection Reasons. However, confidence.level of 62% exceeds the pass threshold of <=50. Given three foundational contradictions, an undefined clock-start, and an incomplete refund matrix, 62% is too high — the agent's own Rejection Reasons section argues the model 'cannot be approved for implementation', which is inconsistent with 62% confidence.

observations.text: PM/Legal 'guarantee vs best-effort' contradiction identified clearly as Contradiction 1 with detailed impact analysis and suggested resolution language
Clock-start ambiguity explicitly called out in Gap G-1 with concrete math (15-min pickup leaves only 1h45m) and elevated to Rejection Reason R-3
Wrong-address refund exception from Finance captured as BR-7 and surfaced in Contradiction 3 as an incomplete refund matrix
Auto-refund vs 3-day processing conflict identified as Contradiction 2 with precise framing of the ambiguity (auto-triggered vs auto-disbursed)
11 business rules extracted with generally correct invariant classifications — exceeds the 5-rule minimum substantially
3 formal Rejection Reasons serve as strong escalations with clear business rule traceability
10 gaps identified with responsible stakeholders — thorough gap analysis beyond what was required
Actionable recommendation to convene alignment session before engineering work begins
Confidence of 62% is too high given the agent's own assessment that the model 'cannot be approved for implementation' with three blocking contradictions — should be <=45 per rubric, <=50 for pass
Contradictions use emoji markers (⛔, ⚠️) rather than formal ESCALATION labels as expected by the rubric, though the Rejection Reasons section compensates
Some assumptions stated in the confidence section silently resolve contradictions (e.g., assumption 3 resolves auto-refund vs 3-day conflict) rather than leaving them fully open

agent_output_excerpt:
## Domain Analysis

### Business Context
This encodes a **time-sensitive premium delivery product** ("Express Delivery") layered on top of an existing standard delivery service. It introduces a new service tier with eligibility constraints, time-bound SLAs, driver qualification requirements, and a conditional refund policy. This is a classic logistics "guaranteed window" offering with significant cross-functional tension on what "guaranteed" actually means.
